title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings s and t of lengths m and n respectively, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t (including duplicates) is included in the window. If there is no such substring, return the empty string "". The testcases will be generated such that the answer is unique.
constraints: s and t consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters
thinking_questions: What makes a window valid? How do you track 'all chars of t are covered'?
Once valid, should you keep expanding or start shrinking?
How do you check 'window contains all of t' in O(1) without scanning the window?

hints.L1: Variable-size window. Expand right until valid (contains all of t), then shrink left to find minimum. Repeat.
hints.L2: t_count map + window map + 'have'/'need' counters. 'have' increments when a char hits its required freq. When have==need, shrink.

follow_up_questions: Why count 'have' based on DISTINCT satisfied chars, not total matched chars?
Key structural difference vs Permutation in String (fixed vs variable window)?
If t has duplicates like 'AAB', how does your solution ensure enough of each?
solution_notes: Variable window. have/need counters. Expand right, shrink left when valid. O(n).
